title: China-to-Thailand EV Charger Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: Connector Compatibility: GB/T 20234 vs IEC 62196 Type 2 / CCS2
rows.target_requirement: Thailand projects, fleets, and charging operators may specify the IEC connector ecosystem: IEC 62196-2:2022 Type 2 for AC charging and IEC 62196-3:2022 Configuration FF, commonly called CCS2, for DC charging. Connector choice is a hardware, communication, vehicle-compatibility, and procurement requirement, not merely a document substitution.
target_standards: IEC 62196-3:2022 Configuration FF / CCS2 DC couplers
Project, operator, fleet, or procurement connector specification
rows.china_equivalent: GB-standard cn_common_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 20234.2 for AC charging interfaces and GB/T 20234.3 for DC charging interfaces. GB/T connector hardware and signalling are not drop-in substitutes for Type 2 or CCS2.
rows.gap: A GB/T-only charger package needs redesign or variant control for Thai IEC-facing deployments: Type 2 or CCS2 couplers, cable thermal rating, locking, proximity and control-pilot circuits, PLC communication where CCS2 is used, labels, spare parts, installation manuals, and retesting after connector changes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory when required by the Thai project, operator, fleet, procurement document, or vehicle-compatibility route; otherwise a market-interoperability requirement rather than standalone legal approval
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 20234 conformity is a China GB-standard connector baseline and is not a substitute for Type 2 or CCS2 compatibility

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not present GB/T 20234 evidence as Type 2 or CCS2 evidence; confirm connector hardware and communication against the Thai deployment specification.

rows.requirement: Electrical Safety, EMC, and RCD / Residual-Current Protection
rows.target_requirement: EV charging equipment for Thailand should be checked against the applicable IEC / TIS safety and EMC parts, including IEC 61851-series safety functions and off-board charger EMC expectations. Installations may also require residual-current protection, earthing, overcurrent protection, isolation, emergency stop, enclosure, and environmental-rating evidence according to the charger type and site design.
target_standards: IEC 61851-21-2:2018 EMC requirements for off-board EV charging systems
Thai electrical installation and utility inspection requirements
rows.china_equivalent: GB-standard cn_common_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 18487 for conductive charging systems and may provide GB/T safety, EMC, leakage-current, insulation, IP, temperature-rise, and protective-function test evidence. That evidence should be mapped clause by clause to Thai IEC / TIS and site requirements.
china_standards: GB/T EMC and electrical safety test evidence
rows.gap: Build a safety file covering RCD or RDC-DD strategy, protective earth continuity, insulation monitoring for DC systems, touch-current limits, EMC test reports, surge protection, emergency stop, enclosure IP / IK rating, Thai installation drawings, and retest triggers after connector or grid-interface changes.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by TISI compulsory scope, electrical installation rules, utility inspection, procurement specification, or site safety approval
mandatory_status.cn: China GB-standard safety and EMC evidence should be mapped and retested where Thai IEC / TIS or installation requirements differ

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Safety and EMC files should show the Thai installation protection concept, not only a China GB/T test report.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 18487 Baseline vs Thailand Route
rows.target_requirement: Thailand-facing evidence should be organized around the Thai route: TISI / IEC standards mapping, connector ecosystem, MEA or PEA site connection, installation safety, and charging-station operating context. A China GB/T 18487 test baseline is useful for engineering comparison but is not a Thai approval route by itself.
target_standards: TISI / IEC EV charging standards mapping
MEA / PEA connection and installation review
Thai charging-station policy and operating requirements
rows.china_equivalent: GB-standard cn_common_equivalent: GB/T 18487 is China's common conductive-charging system baseline, supported by GB/T 20234 for connection devices and GB/T 27930 for off-board DC charger to BMS communication. It should be used as the China-side starting point for a gap matrix.
rows.gap: Convert the China file into a Thai gap matrix: identify clauses that match IEC / TIS, clauses that need retesting after Type 2 or CCS2 conversion, missing Thai-language markings or manuals, site-specific utility documents, and operating-policy documents that are outside the product test report.

mandatory_status.target: Depends on Thai standard scope, site utility approval, installation route, and operating model; no blanket Thai approval follows from GB/T 18487 alone
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T 18487 is a China GB-standard baseline and may be voluntary or procurement-driven depending on the China route and product context

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] GB/T 18487 is the China-side baseline for comparison, not evidence of Thai TISI, MEA, PEA, or operating approval.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation / Standardization Administration of China

rows.requirement: MEA / PEA Grid Connection and Utility Coordination
rows.target_requirement: Thai EV charging sites need utility coordination with MEA in the Bangkok metropolitan service area or PEA in provincial service areas. Site approval may involve service capacity, transformer or service upgrade, metering, tariff category, protection settings, harmonic or power-quality review, installation inspection, and energization procedures.
rows.china_equivalent: GB-standard cn_common_equivalent: China charger documentation often includes GB/T 18487 safety functions and Chinese project electrical drawings. Those documents may support technical review but do not replace MEA or PEA service-connection review for a Thai site.
rows.gap: Prepare Thai site electrical packages: single-line diagrams, load schedule, demand calculation, transformer and service details, earthing, protection coordination, power-quality assumptions, charger load-management logic, commissioning records, and MEA / PEA application documents for the actual service territory.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for site energization and service connection where MEA or PEA approval, inspection, metering, or service upgrade applies
mandatory_status.cn: China GB-standard product evidence does not replace Thai utility service approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Product conformity is separate from Thai utility connection readiness; confirm MEA or PEA requirements for each charging site before installation.

rows.requirement: Charging-Station Policy and EV 3.5 Incentives Context
rows.target_requirement: Thailand's EV market is shaped by national EV policy, investment promotion, excise and subsidy measures, and energy-regulator or utility rules for charging operations. EV 3.5 incentives primarily concern EV market development and vehicle-production / purchase support context; they should not be treated as a product safety approval for imported charging equipment.
target_standards: Thailand national EV policy and investment-promotion measures
Energy regulator and utility requirements for charging-station operation
rows.china_equivalent: GB-standard cn_common_equivalent: China product test evidence under GB/T 18487, GB/T 20234, and GB/T 27930 can support technical due diligence but does not establish Thai policy eligibility, operator licensing, tariff treatment, or incentive status.
rows.gap: Separate product compliance from business-model and incentive eligibility. A charger supplier should confirm whether the Thai customer needs BOI-promoted activity status, ERC or other operating permissions, utility service classification, tariff treatment, data or payment-system requirements, and any procurement condition linked to EV policy.

mandatory_status.target: Policy, licensing, tariff, or incentive dependent; not a substitute for product safety conformity or utility connection approval
mandatory_status.cn: China GB-standard product evidence does not determine Thai incentive or charging-station operating status

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] EV 3.5 and investment-promotion context may affect the project economics, but it should not be cited as charger product approval.

rows.requirement: TISI EV Charging Standards: TIS 61851 Adoption
rows.target_requirement: Thailand uses Thai Industrial Standards issued by TISI. For EV conductive charging equipment, exporters should check the current TISI catalogue and any compulsory-standard notifications for TIS 61851-series adoption of IEC 61851 before claiming Thai conformity. A TIS / IEC technical standard reference is not, by itself, proof that every whole EV charger model is under a blanket compulsory certification route.
target_standards: TISI compulsory standards list and notifications
rows.china_equivalent: GB-standard cn_common_equivalent: China commonly uses GB/T 18487 for conductive charging system requirements together with GB/T 20234 connector standards and GB/T 27930 communication for DC charging. These GB-standard documents support China-market evidence but do not establish TISI acceptance or Thai compulsory-status coverage.
rows.gap: Prepare a Thai standards mapping that separates technical IEC / TIS alignment from legal compulsory certification status. Confirm the exact TIS 61851 part, model rating, AC or DC function, installation use case, marking language, test laboratory route, and whether TISI currently lists the item as compulsory.

mandatory_status.target: Technical standard alignment expected where specified; compulsory status must be confirmed against current TISI compulsory-standard instruments for the exact product and use case
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T evidence is a China GB-standard baseline and does not prove Thai TISI compulsory certification

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat TIS 61851 as a standards-alignment issue first and verify legal compulsory status model by model with TISI before shipment.
